title: cervicina hederacea
intro: Cervicina hederacea, also known as the Bearberry, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to Europe and North America, characterized by its glossy leaves and red berries. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, astringent, and diuretic properties, making it effective in treating urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and other kidney-related issues. The health benefits of Cervicina hederacea include its ability to reduce inflammation, prevent kidney damage, and help with the management of diabetes. Cervicina hederacea can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried extract, often used in traditional medicine to support urinary health and alleviate symptoms associated with kidney problems.
